theme: The bones are solid — now close the gaps and let the small moments sing
visual_observations: Good news first: the heat shimmer on 'on-fire' is already shipping (Office.css lines 81-88 — nice work maya). The char-hover-card is live (Office.jsx line 566, Office.css line 1017) but it's half-finished — missing time-on-task even though worker.updated_at is already available at line 553. The VIBE_WHITEBOARD entry for 'slow-day' in constants.js line 66 literally reads 'TODO: ???' which has escaped notice for multiple reviews. VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES in quotes.js covers 10 of 20 possible transitions — the other half of Bloberto's personality is silent during vibe changes. Plant milestone fires setPlantMilestone(true) in Office.jsx line 834 but the only output is a data-attribute — the user never knows the plant leveled up. Polling silently pauses after IDLE_TIMEOUT (App.jsx line 484) with no visual indication. These are all gaps in features that are nearly complete — each fix is one small step from done.

ideas.title: Complete the Hover Card: Add Time-On-Task
ideas.description:
The char-hover-card (Office.jsx line 566) shows name, role, task, and status — but review #44 explicitly called for 'time on task' and it's still missing. worker.updated_at is already available and formatIdleDuration() is already imported and used for idle workers at line 553. For variant==='working', add a line like
{formatIdleDuration(worker.updated_at)}
inside the .char-hover-card div. Add .char-hover-card .hover-time { opacity: 0.6; font-size: 0.7rem; margin-top: 3px; } to Office.css after line 1040. WHY: The feature was shipped incomplete — the data and the formatting function both exist, the connection was just never made.

ideas.title: Replace Placeholder VIBE_WHITEBOARD 'slow-day' Text
ideas.description: constants.js line 66: VIBE_WHITEBOARD['slow-day'] = 'TODO: ???' — this is a dev placeholder that has shipped to production. Every other vibe key has a real message ('🏆 SHIP IT', '🚨 HELP', '⚡ FLOW', '🌙 ZZZ'). Replace with something intentional, e.g. '📋 BACKLOG?' or '🐢 ...maybe later'. WHY: It literally says TODO in the UI. This is the whiteboard Bloberto stares at all day on slow days — it deserves a real message.

ideas.title: Fill the Missing VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES
ideas.description: quotes.js line 37 defines VIBE_TRANSITION_QUOTES but only 10 of 20 possible transitions are covered (getTeamVibe in vibe.js has 5 outputs: crushing, on-fire, in-flow, slow-day, after-hours → 5×4=20 pairs). Missing pairs include: after-hours→crushing, after-hours→slow-day, after-hours→on-fire, crushing→in-flow, crushing→after-hours, on-fire→slow-day, on-fire→after-hours, slow-day→after-hours, in-flow→after-hours, slow-day→on-fire. Each missing transition means Bloberto says nothing when the vibe shifts — his best comedic moments are at vibe changes. WHY: Half of Bloberto's live commentary is dead air. Sofia can fill these as data, but carlos should wire up the fallback so any unmapped key gets a generic Bloberto line instead of silence.

ideas.title: Polling-Paused Visual Indicator
ideas.description: IDLE_TIMEOUT is 10 minutes (App.jsx line 484). When triggered, the pollTick at line 720 returns early and data goes stale — silently. There's no UI signal to the user. Add an isPollingPaused boolean state derived from lastActivity ref: check it in a useEffect on the same 60s useTimeTick interval. When paused, show a small pill near the sync-now-btn (App.jsx line 979) e.g. 'Paused · move mouse to refresh' styled similarly to .fetch-warn-banner (App.css line 754) but less alarming — maybe just a muted opacity indicator. WHY: A user staring at a frozen 'Crushing It' dashboard while the team has gone to lunch is a trust issue, not just a UX issue.

ideas.title: Plant Level-Up Toast
ideas.description: Office.jsx line 834 calls setPlantMilestone(true) when the ficus advances from one plantStage to the next (seedling→sprout→small-plant→leafy→blooming). Currently the only output is data-milestone on the plant element — zero user-visible feedback. Reuse the exact completionToast pattern from App.jsx line 932: pass a plantLevelUp prop down to Office or emit a callback (like onWorkerClick at line 643), then in App.jsx trigger a toast like '🌱 The office ficus leveled up!' for 3 seconds. WHY: The plant is the team's shared living mascot and its growth milestones are earned through sustained vibes — they deserve the same celebration ceremony as a worker shipping.

ideas.title: Role-Colored Desk Glow Heatmap
ideas.description: ROLE_COLORS in constants.js lines 14-25 defines a rich palette per role but these colors are nearly invisible in the office view — only the avatar circle uses them. For working workers, add a radial-gradient glow beneath each occupied desk using the worker's ROLE_COLORS value. In Office.jsx around the deskOccupants render (~line 960), add an inline style like boxShadow: `0 0 18px 4px ${ROLE_COLORS[occ.worker.role]}33` on the desk element when !occ.ghost && !occ.idle. When the full team is working, the floor would bloom with six soft role-colored halos. Pair with a CSS transition: 0.5s ease for smooth fade-in/out. WHY: Role colors are defined, imported, and forgotten. This makes the office floor read like a living energy map — purple for maya, sky-blue for carlos, orange for dave.
